exports.content: CH3NH3PbI2Cl perovskite film maintains absorbance ~1.8 at 500 nm (98.4% absorption) over 1000 hours of constant simulated full sunlight illumination without UV filtration
exports.label: absorption_stability

exports.content: Replacing mesoporous TiO2 with insulating Al2O3 increases Voc by >200 mV while maintaining comparable Jsc, because electrons are confined in the perovskite rather than injected into the metal oxide
exports.label: al2o3_increases_voc

exports.content: The increased Voc in Al2O3 cells is caused by substantial reduction of chemical capacitance: without mesoporous TiO2 sub-bandgap states, the quasi-Fermi level for electrons moves nearer to the conduction band for the same charge density
exports.label: chemical_capacitance_explanation

exports.content: Is the MSSC fundamentally excitonic or a distributed p-n junction? Do free charges generate in the bulk or are highly mobile excitons quenched at the perovskite-spiro-OMeTAD interface?
exports.label: excitonic_vs_pn

exports.content: Meso-superstructured solar cell (MSSC) using mixed-halide perovskite CH3NH3PbI2Cl processed from DMF precursor via spin-coating in ambient conditions, with either mesoporous TiO2 or insulating Al2O3 scaffold, spiro-OMeTAD hole conductor, and Ag electrode, measured under AM 1.5 100 mW/cm2 simulated sunlight
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: Hole transfer from photoexcited perovskite to spiro-OMeTAD is highly effective in both TiO2 and Al2O3 architectures, confirmed by PIA showing oxidized spiro-OMeTAD features at 525, 750, and 1200 nm
exports.label: hole_transfer_effective

exports.content: The difference between optical bandgap (1.55 eV) and Voc (1.1 V) is only 0.45 eV, competitive with the best thin-film photovoltaic technologies and far superior to typical 0.7-0.8 eV losses in dye-sensitized and organic solar cells
exports.label: low_energy_losses

exports.content: CH3NH3PbI2Cl mixed-halide perovskite with tetragonal structure (a=8.825 A, b=8.835 A, c=11.24 A), long-range crystalline domains >200 nm, and remarkable stability to processing in air, unlike CH3NH3PbI3
exports.label: mixed_halide_perovskite

exports.content: Al2O3-based meso-superstructured solar cell achieves PCE of 10.9% with Jsc=17.8 mA/cm2, Voc=0.98 V, and fill factor 0.63, the highest reported for perovskite or hybrid solar cells at the time
exports.label: pce_10_9_pct

exports.content: The perovskite absorber has conductivity on the order of 10^-3 S/cm, while spiro-OMeTAD is less conductive (~10^-5 S/cm), requiring careful balance between shunt and series resistance
exports.label: perovskite_conductivity

exports.content: The perovskite layer functions as both absorber and n-type semiconductor, transporting electrons through the film thickness, as demonstrated by charge collection faster by a factor >10 in Al2O3 cells vs TiO2 cells
exports.label: perovskite_n_type_transport

exports.content: A planar-junction diode (FTO/TiO2/CH3NH3PbI2Cl/spiro-OMeTAD/Ag) with ~150 nm perovskite film achieves Jsc=7.13 mA/cm2, Voc=0.64 V, FF=0.4, and PCE=1.8%, confirming perovskite can function without mesoporous scaffold
exports.label: planar_junction_feasible

exports.content: Perovskite-sensitized TiO2 solar cell achieves PCE of 7.6% with Jsc=17.8 mA/cm2, Voc=0.80 V, and fill factor 0.53
exports.label: tio2_cell_7_6_pct

exports.content: An Al2O3-based device achieves Voc above 1.13 V with Jsc=15.4 mA/cm2 and fill factor 0.45, yielding 7.8% PCE, demonstrating exceptionally high photovoltage generation
exports.label: voc_above_1_1v
